title: LawnForceMowLetter
description: Validated input data for the 10-day certified force-mow notice (templates/lawn-force-mow-letter.gold-standard.md). Catastrophic-output surface per CLAUDE.md — every render uses this schema for validation.

community_legal_name.description: Full legal name of the HOA. e.g., 'Eaglewood Homeowners' Association, Inc.'

community_short_name.description: Subdivision name as used in the Declaration. e.g., 'Eaglewood'

letter_date.description: Letter date YYYY-MM-DD. Cure clock starts here. Renderer formats as 'April 28, 2026'.

certified_mail_number.description: USPS certified mail tracking number. Optional at draft time, must be entered before actual mailing.

homeowner_names_block.description: Multi-line block: name(s) on line 1, address on subsequent lines.

property_address_full.description: Property address including city/state/zip. e.g., '12345 Bryce Canyon Dr, Richmond, TX 77407'

property_address_short.description: Property address without city/state/zip. Used in tighter contexts.

alt_mailing_address_block.description: Alternative mailing address if owner mailing differs from property. Rendered as a separate block under homeowner_names_block.

declaration_doc_number.description: Recording document number for the Declaration. Per-community.

declaration_county.description: County where the Declaration is recorded. e.g., 'Fort Bend'

declaration_section_full.description: The Declaration section that authorizes force-mow + self-help. Per-community lookup. e.g., 'Article 6.16 of the Declaration'

observation_date.description: Date the violation was observed. From the inspection record.

observed_condition.description: Operator's free-text description of what was observed. e.g., 'Lawn in need of mowing, edging, and weed control'.

include_hearing_rights.description: Whether to include the §209.006-007 hearing rights paragraph. Per Texas law, only required when no notice for the same violation has been sent in the prior six months. Computed by the renderer from violation history.
